text: Here are some best practices for designing REST APIs. First, use clear, logical endpoint names, like `/users` or `/orders`, instead of complex actions. Always use correct HTTP status codes; for example, `201 Created` for a new resource and `404 Not Found` when a resource doesn't exist. Good documentation is essential for helping other developers understand how to use your API. Finally, implement versioning, such as `/v1/users`, so your API can grow and change without breaking existing applications. Following these guidelines will make your API reliable and easy to work with.
